rationale: The brief asks for `useCoins` to expose `lastUpdated`. The hook implementation returns only `{ coins, isLoading }`. The Dashboard consumer destructures `const { coins, isLoading, lastUpdated } = useCoins()`. At runtime, `lastUpdated` is `undefined`, and `formatDate(lastUpdated)` crashes when it calls `.toLocaleString()`. tsc would catch this only if the hook had an explicit return type annotation — agent-generated hooks rarely do. `hook_destructure_consistency` walks every `function/const useX = ... return { … }` (supports concise arrows, block arrows, and traditional function declarations), extracts the LAST top-level return object's keys, then scans `const {…} = useX()` consumers and flags any destructured name not in the returned shape. Hooks whose return shape can't be extracted confidently (spread, computed keys) are dropped from the audit — silence beats false positives.

baseline_comparison.tsc: PARTIAL — catches IF the hook has an explicit return type. Without one (common in agent output), tsc infers the return type from the literal and lets the bug through.
baseline_comparison.eslint-plugin-react-hooks: PASS — the existing rules (exhaustive-deps, rules-of-hooks) don't validate return-shape vs. consumer-destructure.
baseline_comparison.raw_llm_critique: UNRELIABLE — small files: usually catches. Multi-file: misses cross-file return drift.
baseline_comparison.manual_QA: Catches when the page first renders and crashes.
